title: Bright-and-Airy Lightroom Grade for a 38-Shot Food Photography Set (Flat-Lay, 45°, Side)
one_line_ask: Take a client's 38-frame food shoot (flat-lay / 45° / side of one dish) that is close to the mark and grade it to a cohesive bright-and-airy look — white balance, exposure, highlight/shadow, gentle color — then deliver straightened, correctly-sized final files.

issues: Very slight warm shift in the toast crust edges — marginally outside the brief's cool-underexposed target grade, but within acceptable near-miss tolerance
Depth of field could be fractionally shallower to push the espresso cup further into bokeh
Honey drizzle pooling on the plate rim is slightly heavy, may require a minor retouch for cleaner plate edge in final deliverable
Black pepper grind visible on plate surface — minor distraction element the designer may wish to clone out

The image perfectly captures the requested 'source input file' aesthetic, including the deliberate imperfections like a slight cool cast, slightly low exposure, and heavy shadows, which are intended for post-processing. The composition is excellent, and all specified elements are present and well-rendered. It successfully avoids looking like an over-polished render, maintaining a genuine photographic feel.
Exposure is slightly low as intended but shadows under plate rim are marginally heavier than ideal, may need minor lift in post
Honey drizzle sheen is present but not yet luminous — within brief spec
White balance reads faintly cool/neutral rather than warm, consistent with brief
Slight rightward tilt (~1-2°) present — within the ≤5° brief allowance
Microgreens garnish is slightly sparse toward the left edge of the toast
Small espresso cup is partially cropped at upper-right edge, reducing its compositional weight slightly
Slight warm cast on toast crust edges rather than the specified marginally cool white balance — minor, correctable in grade
Window highlights are adequately flat but background bokeh is slightly more diffuse than a true shallow-DOF prime would render — acceptable for source asset
Honey drip pooling on plate is authentic but the drip trail down the toast face could be slightly more pronounced for maximum visual interest at retouching stage
Shadow under toast on plate is moderately heavy as specified — within brief tolerance
No explicit 4000x3000 metadata confirmable from image alone — assume client-supplied at correct resolution
